lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Bhairao, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਦੇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਸਮਾਏ ॥੪॥੯॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: aape guramukh de vaddiaaee naanak naam samaae |4|9|19|
lines.english: He Himself blesses the Gurmukh with glorious greatness; O Nanak, he merges in the Naam. ||4||9||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੀ ਪਟੀਆ ਲਿਖਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਗੋਵਿੰਦ ਗੋਪਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: meree patteea likhahu har govind gopaalaa |
lines.english: Upon my writing tablet, I write the Name of the Lord, the Lord of the Universe, the Lord of the World.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਫਾਥੇ ਜਮ ਜਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: doojai bhaae faathe jam jaalaa |
lines.english: In the love of duality, the mortals are caught in the noose of the Messenger of Death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਮੇਰੈ ਨਾਲਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: har sukhadaataa merai naalaa |1|
lines.english: The Lord, the Giver of peace, is always with me. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਕਰੇ ਮੇਰੀ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kare meree pratipaalaa |
lines.english: The True Guru nurtures and sustains me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਸਨਾ ਤੇ ਬਾਲਕੁ ਗਮੁ ਨ ਕਰੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saasanaa te baalak gam na karai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: he was a child, but he was not afraid when his teacher yelled at him. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸਿ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦੁ ਹਰਿ ਉਚਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur upades prahilaad har ucharai |
lines.english: Following his Guru's instructions, Prahlaad chanted the Lord's Name;

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤਾ ਉਪਦੇਸੈ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: maataa upadesai prahilaad piaare |
lines.english: Prahlaad's mother gave her beloved son some advice:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦੁ ਕਹੈ ਸੁਨਹੁ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: prahilaad kahai sunahu meree maae |
lines.english: Prahlaad said: "Listen, O my mother;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਛੋਡਹੁ ਜੀਉ ਲੇਹੁ ਉਬਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: putr raam naam chhoddahu jeeo lehu ubaare |
lines.english: "My son, you must abandon the Lord's Name, and save your life!"

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਛੋਡਾ ਗੁਰਿ ਦੀਆ ਬੁਝਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: raam naam na chhoddaa gur deea bujhaae |2|
lines.english: I shall never give up the Lord's Name. My Guru has taught me this." ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਡਾ ਮਰਕਾ ਸਭਿ ਜਾਇ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: sanddaa marakaa sabh jaae pukaare |
lines.english: Sandaa and Markaa, his teachers, went to his father the king, and complained:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਸਟ ਸਭਾ ਮਹਿ ਮੰਤ੍ਰੁ ਪਕਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dusatt sabhaa meh mantru pakaaeaa |
lines.english: In the court of the wicked king, a plan was hatched.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦ ਕਾ ਰਾਖਾ ਹੋਇ ਰਘੁਰਾਇਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: prahalaad kaa raakhaa hoe raghuraaeaa |3|
lines.english: God is the Savior of Prahlaad. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦੁ ਆਪਿ ਵਿਗੜਿਆ ਸਭਿ ਚਾਟੜੇ ਵਿਗਾੜੇ ॥
lines.translit: prahilaad aap vigarriaa sabh chaattarre vigaarre |
lines.english: "Prahlaad himself has gone astray, and he leads all the other pupils astray."

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਤੇਰਾ ਕਹਾ ਤੁਝੁ ਲਏ ਉਬਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: har teraa kahaa tujh le ubaar |
lines.english: "Where is your Lord, who will save you?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਾਥਿ ਖੜਗੁ ਕਰਿ ਧਾਇਆ ਅਤਿ ਅਹੰਕਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: haath kharrag kar dhaaeaa at ahankaar |
lines.english: With sword in hand, and with great egotistical pride, Prahlaad's father ran up to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਣਾਖਸੁ ਨਖੀ ਬਿਦਾਰਿਆ ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦੁ ਲੀਆ ਉਬਾਰਿ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: haranaakhas nakhee bidaariaa prahalaad leea ubaar |4|
lines.english: Harnaakhash was torn apart by His claws, and Prahlaad was saved. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿਨ ਮਹਿ ਭੈਆਨ ਰੂਪੁ ਨਿਕਸਿਆ ਥੰਮੑ ਉਪਾੜਿ ॥
lines.translit: khin meh bhaiaan roop nikasiaa thama upaarr |
lines.english: In an instant, the Lord appeared in a dreadful form, and shattered the pillar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦ ਜਨ ਕੇ ਇਕੀਹ ਕੁਲ ਉਧਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: prahalaad jan ke ikeeh kul udhaare |
lines.english: He saved twenty-one generations of Prahlaad's descendents.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਕੇ ਹਰਿ ਜੀਉ ਕਾਰਜ ਸਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: sant janaa ke har jeeo kaaraj savaare |
lines.english: The Dear Lord completes the tasks of the Saints.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਬਿਖੁ ਮਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sabad haumai bikh maare |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, the poison of egotism is neutralized.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Bhairao, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਦੈਤ ਲਾਇ ਦਿਤੇ ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਕਉ ਆਪੇ ਰਾਖਾ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: aape dait laae dite sant janaa kau aape raakhaa soee |
lines.english: The Lord Himself makes demons pursue the Saints, and He Himself saves them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੰਤ ਨਿਸਤਾਰੇ ॥੫॥੧੦॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: naanak raam naam sant nisataare |5|10|20|
lines.english: O Nanak, through the Name of the Lord, the Saints are emancipated. ||5||10||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੁਗਿ ਜੁਗਿ ਭਗਤਾ ਕੀ ਰਖਦਾ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jug jug bhagataa kee rakhadaa aaeaa |
lines.english: In each and every age, the Lord saves the honor of His devotees.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤੇਰੀ ਸਦਾ ਸਰਣਾਈ ਤਿਨ ਮਨਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jo teree sadaa saranaaee tin man dukh na hoee |1|
lines.english: Those who remain forever in Your Sanctuary, O Lord - their minds are never touched by sorrow. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੈਤ ਪੁਤ੍ਰੁ ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦੁ ਗਾਇਤ੍ਰੀ ਤਰਪਣੁ ਕਿਛੂ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਸਬਦੇ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਇਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: dait putru prahalaad gaaeitree tarapan kichhoo na jaanai sabade mel milaaeaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Prahlaad, the demon's son, knew nothing of the Hindu morning prayer, the Gayatri, and nothing about ceremonial water-offerings to his ancestors; but through the Word of the Shabad, he was united in the Lord's Union. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਦਿਨ ਰਾਤੀ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਸਬਦੇ ਖੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: anadin bhagat kareh din raatee dubidhaa sabade khoee |
lines.english: Night and day, he performed devotional worship service, day and night, and through the Shabad, his duality was eradicated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਹੈ ਜੋ ਸਚਿ ਰਾਤੇ ਸਚੁ ਵਸਿਆ ਮਨਿ ਸੋਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sadaa niramal hai jo sach raate sach vasiaa man soee |2|
lines.english: Those who are imbued with Truth are immaculate and pure; the True Lord abides within their minds. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਪੜ੍ਹਹਿ ਮੂਲੁ ਨ ਪਛਾਣਹਿ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: moorakh dubidhaa parrheh mool na pachhaaneh birathaa janam gavaaeaa |
lines.english: The fools in duality read, but they do not understand anything; they waste their lives uselessly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਕੀ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕਰਹਿ ਦੁਸਟੁ ਦੈਤੁ ਚਿੜਾਇਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sant janaa kee nindaa kareh dusatt dait chirraaeaa |3|
lines.english: The wicked demon slandered the Saint, and stirred up trouble. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦੁ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨ ਪੜੈ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਛੋਡੈ ਡਰੈ ਨ ਕਿਸੈ ਦਾ ਡਰਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: prahalaad dubidhaa na parrai har naam na chhoddai ddarai na kisai daa ddaraaeaa |
lines.english: Prahlaad did not read in duality, and he did not abandon the Lord's Name; he was not afraid of any fear.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਕਾ ਹਰਿ ਜੀਉ ਰਾਖਾ ਦੈਤੈ ਕਾਲੁ ਨੇੜਾ ਆਇਆ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: sant janaa kaa har jeeo raakhaa daitai kaal nerraa aaeaa |4|
lines.english: The Dear Lord became the Savior of the Saint, and the demonic Death could not even approach him. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣੀ ਪੈਜ ਆਪੇ ਰਾਖੈ ਭਗਤਾਂ ਦੇਇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: aapanee paij aape raakhai bhagataan dee vaddiaaee |
lines.english: The Lord Himself saved his honor, and blessed his devotee with glorious greatness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਣਾਖਸੁ ਨਖੀ ਬਿਦਾਰਿਆ ਅੰਧੈ ਦਰ ਕੀ ਖਬਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥੫॥੧੧॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: naanak haranaakhas nakhee bidaariaa andhai dar kee khabar na paaee |5|11|21|
lines.english: O Nanak, Harnaakhash was torn apart by the Lord with His claws; the blind demon knew nothing of the Lord's Court. ||5||11||21||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਸੰਤ ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਪਗਿ ਲਾਇਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: har jan sant kar kirapaa pag laaein |
lines.english: The Lord, in His Mercy, attaches mortals to the feet of the Saints.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੪ ਚਉਪਦੇ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: raag bhairau mahalaa 4 chaupade ghar 1 |
lines.english: Raag Bhairao, Fourth Mehl, Chau-Padhay, First House:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
